description:
You are a localization engineer who has just cloned a small demo project located in
/home/user/project/

It already contains two configuration files:

1. /home/user/project/translations.yml  
2. /home/user/project/settings.toml  

Current contents of /home/user/project/translations.yml (YAML, 2-space indentation):
-------------------------------------------------
en:
  login: "Login"
  logout: "Logout"

fr:
  login: "Connexion"
  logout: "Déconnexion"
-------------------------------------------------

Current contents of /home/user/project/settings.toml:
-------------------------------------------------
[localization]
default_locale = "en_US"
fallback_locale = "en_US"
-------------------------------------------------

Your task (two short edits and one small log):

A. In /home/user/project/translations.yml  
   • Add a brand-new Spanish (es) section directly below the French block.  
   • The Spanish section must contain exactly two keys, login and logout, with the values shown below.  
   • Keep the existing 2-space indentation style intact.  

   Required final YAML block:
   es:
     login: "Iniciar sesión"
     logout: "Cerrar sesión"

B. In /home/user/project/settings.toml  
   • Change the value of default_locale so it reads:
     default_locale = "es_ES"
   • Do NOT modify fallback_locale or any other lines.

C. Create (or overwrite if it already exists) a plain-text log file called  
   /home/user/update_log.txt  

   It must contain exactly three lines, no leading or trailing spaces, Unix line-feeds (`\n`), and **nothing else**:

   translations.yml modified
   settings.toml modified
   Spanish localization added

Important notes the automatic grader will check:
• File paths and names must match **exactly**.  
• The YAML indentation must remain 2 spaces for every nested level.  
• The TOML file may keep its original spacing, except for the single value change requested.  
• The log file must contain exactly the three lines shown (case-sensitive), in that exact order, with no extra blank lines.

You should be able to accomplish everything with about 2-3 shell commands (append, sed, printf, etc.). When you are done, the system should reflect the precise final state described above.
truth:
Initial files (already present before the agent starts):

1. Path: /home/user/project/translations.yml  
   Content:
en:
  login: "Login"
  logout: "Logout"

fr:
  login: "Connexion"
  logout: "Déconnexion"

2. Path: /home/user/project/settings.toml  
   Content:
[localization]
default_locale = "en_US"
fallback_locale = "en_US"

Target state after agent completes:

1. /home/user/project/translations.yml must be exactly:
en:
  login: "Login"
  logout: "Logout"

fr:
  login: "Connexion"
  logout: "Déconnexion"

es:
  login: "Iniciar sesión"
  logout: "Cerrar sesión"

2. /home/user/project/settings.toml must be exactly:
[localization]
default_locale = "es_ES"
fallback_locale = "en_US"

3. /home/user/update_log.txt must exist with content (including terminating newline at end of each line):
translations.yml modified\n
settings.toml modified\n
Spanish localization added\n
